qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of service via panic from unsafe string split operation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Identifies the same vulnerable function (RechargePut), same vulnerable code location (internal/sbi/api_convergedcharging.go:175-178), same root cause (unsafe strings.Split without bounds checking causing index out of range panic), and same security consequence (DoS through service panic) as the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Hardcoded FTP credentials allow unauthorized access to CDR files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class (hardcoded credentials vs out-of-bounds slice access), different affected component (FTP server in internal/cgf/cgf.go vs RechargePut handler in internal/sbi/api_convergedcharging.go), and different security consequence (unauthorized data access vs denial of service).

selected_finding.title: Denial of service via panic from unsafe string split operation

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The dos-via-unsafe-split finding identifies the same vulnerable code and root cause as the advisory: the RechargePut handler in internal/sbi/api_convergedcharging.go performs an unsafe strings.Split operation on the rechargingInfo parameter and directly accesses ueIdRatingGroup[1] without checking the slice length, causing an index out of range panic when the input lacks an underscore delimiter. This matches the advisory's description of the out-of-bounds slice access vulnerability leading to denial of service. Tier3 because the PoC reproduction bundle exists but cannot be executed: the run.sh script requires a target repository checkout mounted at /target-repo, which is not present in the session folder, preventing runtime verification of the exploit.
evidence: Finding identifies same vulnerable endpoint: PUT /nchf-convergedcharging/v3/recharging/:rechargingInfo
Finding describes same root cause: strings.Split(rechargingInfo, "_") followed by direct ueIdRatingGroup[1] access without bounds check
Finding describes same security consequence: index out of range panic causing denial of service
Advisory references align: finding matches the vulnerability described in GHSA-6g43-577r-wf4x, CVE-2026-32937, and free5gc/chf/pull/61
No runtime execution possible: session folder lacks repository checkout required by reproduction/run.sh
blockers_to_higher_tier: Missing repository checkout: reproduction/run.sh requires target repo mounted at /target-repo but session has no repo directory
No runtime evidence available to distinguish Tier1/Tier2: cannot verify actual panic behavior or service crash
Static analysis only: finding relies on source code pattern matching without executable PoC demonstration
